title: eryngium humile var. radiciflorum
intro: Eryngium humile var. radiciflorum, also known as sea holly, is a flowering plant species in the carrot family, characterized by its blue or white flowers and spiky foliage. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Eryngium humile var. radiciflorum include its potential to reduce swelling and pain, promote wound healing, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to create teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
